subject: mathematics
title: Combine and Separate Quantities
description: Give students opportunities to separate and combine quantities using a variety of concrete materials and explain their thinking. This builds foundational understanding of addition and subtraction concepts.

subject: mathematics
title: Multiple Representation Story Problems
description: Present story problem structures for multiple representations. Example: "There are 5 pieces of fruit in a bowl. If there are two kinds of fruit, what might they be and how many of each?" Students explore different ways to partition 5.

subject: mathematics
title: Missing Value Problems
description: Solve missing value problems using manipulatives. Example: "If there are 12 counters in a bag, some red and some blue, and 8 are red, how many are blue?" Students use concrete materials to find the unknown.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Line Walking Game
description: Use games to demonstrate how addition and subtraction relate to each other. Students physically walk on a large floor number line, moving forward for addition and backward for subtraction.

subject: mathematics
title: Outdoor Collection and Comparison
description: Collect materials outside and compare, combine, and separate quantities. Use leaves, shells, popsicle sticks, and bottle caps. Connect to data collection activities.

subject: mathematics
title: Taking Away Action Songs
description: Sing action songs that focus on taking away or removing. Songs like "Ten Little Monkeys" and "Ten Green Bottles" help students understand subtraction through music and movement.

subject: mathematics
title: Pan Scale Comparison
description: Use a pan scale to show different amounts and discuss which is larger, smaller, and what is the difference. Students physically see the concept of comparing quantities.

subject: mathematics
title: Tower Building Comparison
description: Make towers with blocks to discuss which is taller, shorter, and find the difference. Count the blocks to quantify the comparison.

subject: mathematics
title: Height Comparison Activity
description: Compare heights of students or objects (link to measurement). Use non-standard units to find the difference between heights.

subject: mathematics
title: Story Problem Acting
description: Play with sets of objects to act out combining, separating, and comparing. Model different story problems to find the missing value through dramatic play.

subject: mathematics
title: Inverse Relationship Demonstration
description: Show how addition and subtraction undo each other. Example: 6 + 3 = 9 and 9 – 3 = 6. Use manipulatives to demonstrate that 3 + 2 = 5 then 5 – 3 = 2.

subject: mathematics
title: Fact Family Exploration
description: Look at a group of numbers and say how they are related. Example fact family: 4 + 3 = 7, 3 + 4 = 7, 7 – 3 = 4, 7 – 4 = 3. Complete fact families vertically and horizontally from given diagrams.

subject: mathematics
title: Diagram to Equation
description: Introduce using a diagram or picture to communicate an addition equation. Record what the diagram shows by writing the number sentence.

subject: mathematics
title: Counter Diagram Recording
description: Use diagrams with counters: "How many blue counters? How many red counters? 5 and 3 make how many counters?" Record equation 5 + 3 = 8.

subject: mathematics
title: Counting On Vocabulary Building
description: Build vocabulary: "a way to add is by counting on from a given number" (6 + 2 = 8). Practice counting on strategy with manipulatives.

subject: mathematics
title: Counting Back Vocabulary Building
description: Build vocabulary: "a way to subtract is by counting backward from the greater number" (5 – 2 = 3). Practice counting back strategy.

subject: mathematics
title: Fact Family Brainstorm
description: Brainstorm that a fact family includes all addition and subtraction facts using the same numbers. Create fact family houses or triangles.

subject: mathematics
title: Tens Frame Subitizing
description: Place counters in a tens frame (any amount up to ten) - supports subitising. Students quickly recognize quantities without counting.

subject: mathematics
title: Tens Frame Counting On
description: Place red counters in tens frame, ask how many, then add different coloured counters. Use two different colours to encourage counting on and support subitising.

subject: mathematics
title: Bus Simulation Activity
description: Use caution tape to model a bus with 16 spaces (wrapping around seats). Probe students: "How many passengers can the bus hold? How do you know?" Place students in the taped area and ask: "How many passengers on the bus? How many more needed to fill it?"

subject: mathematics
title: Tens Frame Story Completion
description: Make copies of tens frames for students to complete addition equations for given stories. Explore number bonds to 10 - different number combinations that add up to 10.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Line Story Representation
description: Allow students to place manipulatives on a number line to represent story problems. Use number line to represent addition problems up to 20 (concrete to abstract).

subject: mathematics
title: Number Strip Counting On
description: Use counters on number strips. Example: 4 red counters, then add 3 different coloured counters. Students count on to find the total.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Grid Subtraction Game
description: Work in pairs using number grids to answer subtraction questions by counting back. Partners take turns creating and solving subtraction problems.

subject: mathematics
title: Egg Carton Addition
description: Using an egg carton with a given amount, find out how many are needed to fill it, then write the number sentence. Work on simple "change unknown" problems (e.g., 5 + □ = 12).

subject: mathematics
title: Subtraction Terminology Practice
description: Link the terms "take away" and "minus" when writing subtraction equations. Use phrases such as "the difference between" to help compare quantities.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Grid Subtraction Dice Game
description: Use number grid with coloured counter - place counter on called number, roll die to subtract, move counter back. Practice counting back strategy.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Bonds to Ten Video
description: Watch "Number Bonds to Ten Song" - I Know My Number Bonds 10 (Jack Hartmann). Sing along and practice number bonds through music.

subject: mathematics
title: Count and Combine Objects
description: Count and combine objects into different groups and move them around to find the answer. Show the part + part = whole using small objects to practice solving problems.

subject: mathematics
title: Bowling Pin Subtraction
description: Set up 20 bowling pins (or cups) and use a ball to knock some down. Record subtraction equations to represent what is left standing (i.e., 20 pins – ? = ?).

subject: mathematics
title: Decomposing Numbers
description: Practice decomposing whole numbers into various parts using concrete objects and record using symbols. Represent number sentences after decomposing using numerals and symbols.

subject: mathematics
title: Concrete to Symbolic Progression
description: Use concrete models (blocks), drawings, and then symbols (numbers) to represent number facts. Progress from manipulatives to pictorial to abstract representations.

subject: mathematics
title: Storyboard Recording
description: Use storyboards to add/subtract items and record results. Students create visual stories then translate to number sentences.

subject: mathematics
title: Set to Symbol Matching
description: Match the set to its number symbol. Students connect quantities to their numeric representation.

subject: mathematics
title: Addition Symbol Discussion
description: Class discussion on the symbol (+) of addition. Teacher demonstrates/prompts as students come up with their own words that tell (+) addition.

subject: mathematics
title: Symbolic Representation Cards
description: In small groups, give cards or manipulatives to write symbolic representation of what is on the card or what they made. Transfer pictorial to symbolic representation.

subject: mathematics
title: Write the Number Symbol
description: Write the number (symbol) to the set/group shown. Use symbols to represent the pictures shown on worksheets.

subject: mathematics
title: Dice Symbolic Representation
description: In pairs, small groups or whole class - roll two dice, then write symbolic representations showing additive thinking. Record equations like 3 + 5 = 8.

subject: mathematics
title: Draw and Match Activity
description: Draw any amount of objects in circles then write the correct number symbol to match. Self-evaluation activity.

subject: mathematics
title: Extension: Numbers Beyond 20
description: Challenge activity: Students needing extension move to numbers greater than 20 (e.g., show 23 + 17 =). Match pictorial to symbolic representation.

subject: mathematics
title: Language Integration: Number Sentences
description: Language/Reading integration: Write sentences about pictures with numbers (e.g., "Mum has ___ bananas"). Include number names during vocabulary lessons (zero to twenty).

subject: mathematics
title: Social Studies: Family Numbers
description: Social Science integration: Draw family and different family types, write number for total family members. Connect math to real-life contexts.

subject: mathematics
title: Science: Body Parts Counting
description: Science integration: Draw and write numbers for body parts (toes, eyes, ears, fingers, nose). Extension: "All of your fingers + all of your best friends fingers."

subject: mathematics
title: Equal Groups Exploration
description: Provide opportunities to work with equal group problems involving three quantities: number of groups, size of each group, and total of all groups. Form groups using 20 objects (advanced learners can exceed 20).

subject: mathematics
title: Multiplication as Repeated Addition
description: Make the connection that multiplication and repeated addition produce the same results. Examples: 1 × 3 means add 3 one time; 2 × 3 means add 3 two times (2 × 3 = 3 + 3 = 6); 3 × 2 means add 2 three times (3 × 2 = 2 + 2 + 2 = 6).

subject: mathematics
title: Story Problems with Multiplication
description: Write story problems using the multiplication sign and explain what the two factors mean. Example: 4 × 8 refers to 4 sets of 8.

subject: mathematics
title: Building Groups with Manipulatives
description: Use manipulatives to build groups (e.g., group 20 objects into 2s, 3s, 4s...10s). Count the number of groups formed and the total number of objects used. Remaining objects that dont form a complete group can be placed aside.

subject: mathematics
title: Skip Count and Record
description: Example: 20 objects into groups of 3s = 6 groups of 3, skip count to find total of 18. Write mathematical statements: 3+3+3+3+3+3 = 18; "six groups of three make 18".

subject: mathematics
title: Equal Groups Video
description: Watch YouTube video on grouping objects and writing repeated addition equations. "Show Equal Groups with Repeated Addition" video.

subject: mathematics
title: Multiplication as Repeated Addition Video
description: Watch "Multiplication as Repeated Addition" video (goes up to 40, can pause to suit grade level). Students observe and practice along.

subject: mathematics
title: Picture Skip Counting
description: Count items in pictures and skip count to find totals. Skip count to find the total of each grouping shown on worksheets.

subject: mathematics
title: Array Building
description: In small groups, use objects to form arrays. Example: Make 3 groups of 4 - place 3 objects in a row, fill each row with 3 additional objects until 3 rows with 4 objects each. Skip count the total and write mathematical statement: 4+4+4=12, "three groups of 4 make 12".

subject: mathematics
title: Musical Chairs Grouping Game
description: Teacher rolls die and calls out a number for grouping. Music starts, students form groups of that number before music ends. Students not in a group when music ends are out. After each grouping: count students in group, number of groups in class, and total.

subject: mathematics
title: Equal Sharing Discovery
description: Place learners in pairs with even number of items. Discover how many ways items can be shared equally (e.g., 20 items: 2s, 4s, 5s, 10s). Fill out cards for each equal group made.

subject: mathematics
title: Fair Sharing (Partition Division)
description: Solve problems where group size is unknown. Example: "John has 20 apples. He wants to give equal shares to his 4 friends. How many apples will each friend receive?" Students use manipulatives to solve.

subject: mathematics
title: Repeated Subtraction Division
description: Solve problems where number of groups is unknown but size is known. Example: "John has 20 apples. He put them into bags containing 5 apples each. How many bags did John use?"

subject: mathematics
title: Division Mathematical Statements
description: Write mathematical statements to represent division. Example: "20 counters shared into 5s give 4 groups". Circle groups of items equally on activity sheets.

subject: mathematics
title: Group Counting and Recording
description: Write total number of groups in each case. Count total items, say how many groups formed when shared. Example: "2 bears shared into 2s is 1 group".

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Song Introduction
description: Allow learners to listen/view and participate in a pattern song. At the end of the video, pose questions at various cognitive levels to help learners recall, describe/explain, reproduce and extend the pattern.

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Video Discussion
description: After watching pattern video, ask questions like: "What did you observe about the cars in the video? How do you call what you observed?" Encourage learners to describe patterns they noticed.

subject: mathematics
title: Cut-out Pattern Activity
description: In pairs, learners use their cut-outs or cars to demonstrate their solutions to questions being asked. Arrange cars in the order they appeared in the video. Identify which car came first, which came after the red car.

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Prediction
description: Students predict which car/object will come next based on pattern arrangements. Teacher guides discussion about pattern rules and extensions.

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Core Identification
description: Look at the pattern core and identify terms: "Each car is called a term. How many terms are in the pattern core?" Identify the rule of the pattern from the pattern core.

subject: mathematics
title: Extending Patterns
description: Follow the rule and make the pattern longer using cut-outs. Find different ways to extend the pattern (can extend from any direction). Identify which element is missing and explain how they knew.

subject: mathematics
title: Exploring Patterns - Think, Pair, Share
description: Provide opportunities to explore, create and talk about repeating patterns with two, three, and four-element cores progressively. In pairs or small groups, rotate through different activity centers to create patterns. Share creations with partner/group members.

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Questioning
description: Ask questions to help conceptualize characteristics of a repeating pattern: "Can you show me your pattern core?" "How many elements make up your pattern core?" "How are you sure that you have a pattern?" "Can you extend your pattern?"

subject: mathematics
title: Manipulative Center - Pattern Creation
description: Use natural and found materials to create patterns: leaves, sticks, pebbles, sea shells, building blocks, toys including soft toys, slices of plantains/potatoes, cotton buds.

subject: mathematics
title: Art Center - Pattern Printing
description: Create patterns using art materials: water, paint, stones, different drawing materials, sponges, leaves, pebbles. Print patterns using stamps made from natural materials.

subject: mathematics
title: Music Center - Sound Patterns
description: Create sound patterns using tins, bottle spoons, wooden sticks, shak-shak (homemade rattles). Students create and repeat rhythmic patterns.

subject: mathematics
title: Physical Education Center - Movement Patterns
description: Use students' own body movements to create patterns. Use pictures of different body movements to sequence and create patterns.

subject: mathematics
title: AB Pattern Creation
description: Create patterns using ABABAB format (2 elements in the core). Practice with various materials and discuss the pattern rule.

subject: mathematics
title: ABC Pattern Creation
description: Create patterns using ABCABCABC format (3 elements in the core). Extend to patterns containing up to four element cores (ABCD ABCD ABCD format).

subject: mathematics
title: Geoboard Shape Creation
description: Have students use rubber bands to create shapes on geoboard. Explore different shapes and their properties.

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Translation Circle
description: Gather children around a physical circle with different types of materials. Direct attention to a repeating AB letter pattern on cardboard in the center. Reproduce the pattern and demonstrate sound and movement patterns that match.

subject: mathematics
title: Multi-Modal Pattern Translation
description: Create and translate patterns from one form/mode to another: Body parts to words (foot foot hand), Action to shapes (stump stump snap), Letters to objects/shapes (AAB AAB AAB). Lead learners to see that attributes can change without changing structure.

subject: mathematics
title: Talking Circles - Pattern Translation
description: Students explore patterns, change attributes and engage in translating them in circles. Discuss observations and share with each other. Use actions, letters, and physical objects to create repeating patterns.

subject: mathematics
title: Choral Pattern Chanting
description: One group sings/chants a pattern (ABCABCABC). Other group responds/translates by saying/singing their selected pattern (hop jump skip hop jump skip). Practice with formats: ABABAB, AABAABAAB, etc.

subject: mathematics
title: Robocop Error Spotting
description: Invite learners to take on the role of Robocop to spot errors in patterns. Present a sequence of objects, drawings or pictures to pairs/small groups. Learners put on "Robocop hats" before beginning the activity.

subject: mathematics
title: Error Analysis Discussion
description: Study the arrangement of objects and answer: "What do you observe?" "Does it show a pattern?" "Can you describe the rule?" "Is there any object that does not belong?" Work in pairs to spot and correct errors, share thinking with partners.

subject: mathematics
title: Border Pattern Creation
description: Create border patterns in groups with 2-4 elements in the core for their desks. Source items from home to create patterns. Given 3 shapes, create borders with shapes arranged differently for each desk.

subject: mathematics
title: Increasing Pattern Body Movements
description: Learners listen to a chant by the teacher and demonstrate body movements to reflect a given increasing pattern (ABBABBABB). Use physical movements, steps, hand movements or full body movements (e.g., up down down).

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Movement Reflection
description: Reflect using guiding questions: "What did you notice while you were doing the actions?" "Can you tell me what action we should do next?" "Are you sure this is what comes next? Why?" "Can you continue the pattern?"

subject: mathematics
title: Growing Pattern with Blocks
description: Present learners with a sequence made out of blocks, bottle caps or physical objects showing increasing pattern (ABABBABBB). Discussion: "What do you notice?" "Is there a pattern?" "What comes next? Why?"

subject: mathematics
title: Creating Own Growing Patterns
description: After extending the pattern, create a sequence reflecting their own pattern using coloured blocks, bottle caps, emoticons or physical objects.

subject: mathematics
title: Peter's Missing Buttons Story
description: Listen to the story about Peter's Missing Buttons where shirts have increasing buttons (1, 2, 3, 4, 5). Ask questions: "How many buttons do you think will be on the third shirt, 4th shirt, 5th shirt?" Extension: "If Peter had pulled out shirt number 10, how many buttons?"

subject: mathematics
title: Visualizing the Button Story
description: Draw or use objects representing the 5 shirts based on what was heard. Explain the patterns observed in the story.

subject: mathematics
title: Drawing Growing/Decreasing Patterns
description: Draw, paint, create an increasing or decreasing pattern by 1 or 2 elements. Use patterned sponges or leaves or items found in the environment.

subject: mathematics
title: Manipulative Growing Patterns
description: Demonstrate growing/increasing/decreasing patterns using cut outs, bottle caps, blocks or cubes. Build patterns that grow by 1 or 2 each time.

subject: mathematics
title: Growing Pattern Error Analysis
description: Present students with patterned sequences to analyze. Determine whether patterns follow a given sequence. Spot errors and give reasons why an error exists.

subject: mathematics
title: Error Correction Discussion
description: Determine what needs to be changed or adjusted in the sequence. Guided questions: "Is there a pattern?" "Does the pattern continue in a sequence?" "Can you identify the element that is missing?" "Can you fix the error?"

subject: mathematics
title: Building Towers with Connecting Cubes
description: Present learners with a set of connecting cubes. Ask students to build a tower of six, then build a tower of two cubes. Question students: "How many more or fewer?" (e.g., "4 more to make 9").

subject: mathematics
title: Part-Part-Whole with Number Frames
description: Provide opportunities to use materials to represent Part-Part-Whole relationships. Use Five Frames and Ten Frames to model unknowns. Give students number frames with some coloured counters.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Frame Questioning
description: Question students about the number of counters and how many more are missing to form the quantity. As students share responses, model using different coloured counters and place the missing part. Example: "This ten-frame has 6 counters. How many more are missing to make 10?"

subject: mathematics
title: Box Diagrams for Unknowns
description: Use a box diagram as alternative strategy (e.g., 7 = 3 + ?). Draw box diagrams to represent part-part-whole relationships with unknowns.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Lines for Unknowns
description: Use number lines to show unknown numbers. Visualize unknowns on number lines by jumping forward or backward.

subject: mathematics
title: Story Problems - Result Unknown
description: Listen to or read stories involving join, separate, part-part-whole and comparing problem structures. Example: "Sam has 8 sweets. Gina gave him some more. Sam now has 10 sweets. How many sweets did Gina give to Sam?" (8 + ____ = 10)

subject: mathematics
title: Story Problems - Start Unknown
description: Solve stories where the starting amount is unknown. Example: "Sam has some sweets. Gina gave him 2 sweets. Sam now has 10 sweets. How many sweets did Sam have at first?" (____ + 2 = 10)

subject: mathematics
title: Mental Math Starters
description: Relate situations where students use manipulatives or drawings to represent situations. Examples: "I think of a number. I added 3, I now have 7. What number did I think of?" "My number is 8. I subtract a certain number from it, I now have 4. What number did I subtract?"

subject: mathematics
title: Joining and Separating with Drawings
description: Represent the unknown using joining and separating situations using drawings/pictures. Examples: 4 + 3 = _____, 4 - ____ = 3, ____ - 1 = 3

subject: mathematics
title: Finding Unknowns with Inverse Operations
description: Find unknown quantities using the inverse of the operation. Use concrete materials and pictorial representations. Examples: 5 + ___ = 6 → 6 - 5 = ____, ____ - 3 = 4 → 4 + 3 = ____

subject: mathematics
title: Number Combinations Song
description: View a video on number combinations with the sum of ten and sing along (https://youtu.be/n2Nfez2e67k?t=65). Apply the same rhythm and melody to other number combinations made for other sums.

subject: mathematics
title: Classifying Equal Lengths
description: Classify objects based on equal lengths (building blocks, strips, strings, etc.). Given a pair of lengths, make them equal to each other.

subject: mathematics
title: Creating Equal Lengths
description: Use objects to create equal lengths (string equal lengths of beads, stack equal lengths of blocks). Match equal lengths using letters assigned to each length on a grid (e.g., D = F).

subject: mathematics
title: Building Equivalent Lengths
description: In pairs, create equal lengths using building blocks for given lengths. Stack blocks one at a time keeping in mind the previous number. Add blocks to shorter length or remove blocks from longer length to make equivalent.

subject: mathematics
title: Domino Equal Combinations
description: Solve stories involving equal number combinations. Example: "Mary needs to find all the dominoes in the case with combinations adding up to 8. Which dominoes must she take?"

subject: mathematics
title: Card Equal Combinations
description: Example: "Jane has to make other equal combinations of cards of a given suit. She is given 2 of hearts and 5 of hearts. What combinations will Jane make?"

subject: mathematics
title: Creating Number Combinations
description: In small groups, create different number combinations for a given example. Follow illustrated patterns to explain equivalent combinations created. Examples for 5: 5 and 0 make 5, 3 and 2 make 5, 4 and 1 make 5.

subject: mathematics
title: Playing Card Equivalence
description: Create other number combinations equal to a given number combination using playing cards. Use guess and check method to make equivalent playing card combinations. In groups, discuss and compare responses.

subject: mathematics
title: Money Equivalence Problem
description: Example: "Mark has 2 five-dollar notes. Sue has 1 five-dollar note and 3 one-dollar coins. How much more money does Sue need to make the two amounts equal?" Complete diagrams to make amounts equal.

subject: mathematics
title: Solid Figure Construction
description: Have students use cut outs to make solid figures. Students use given solid figures and make a completely new object.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Line Equivalence
description: Use number lines to create equivalent combinations. Complete combinations for given combinations on number lines. Compare three given number lines to identify pairs showing equal number combinations.

subject: mathematics
title: Dominoes - Odd One Out
description: Given a set of dominoes, find the "odd one out". State reasons for choosing equal number combinations. State reasons for not choosing the domino that doesn't belong.

subject: mathematics
title: True or False Equivalence
description: Analyze pairs of number combinations and check true or false for equality. Discuss how to make non-equivalent combinations equal to others.

subject: mathematics
title: Pan Balance Equations
description: Use pan balance or arm balance to understand the concept of a balanced equation. Manipulate coloured counters of equal size to form as many equations as possible. Stick an "=" sign in the middle of the balance.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Bonds for Equations
description: Use number bonds to develop understanding of commutative property of addition (2 + 3 = 3 + 2). Understand part-part-whole relationship of numbers.

subject: mathematics
title: Concrete to Abstract Equations
description: Use counters and cards to make connection from concrete to abstract when forming equations.

subject: mathematics
title: Block Tower Equations
description: Use stackable blocks of different colours (same size) to form equations. Form two stacks with eight blocks each. Manipulate stacks with different colours to form different equations using fact families. Examples: 7 + 1 = 8, 3 + 5 = 8, 6 + 2 = 8.

subject: mathematics
title: Frame Equations
description: Master the use of 5 frames then use 10 frames to formulate number sentences. Manipulate frames to formulate as many equations as possible.

subject: mathematics
title: Number Sentence Circle Game
description: Pull chairs/desks in a physical circle. Teacher calls out (or writes) three numbers. Students formulate a number sentence using all three numbers. First person to answer gets to hold a ball. Ball passes to next person who answers correctly.

subject: mathematics
title: Fishing for Equations
description: Play fishing games in small groups to collect numbers that make number sentences true. Discuss what is needed to make number sentences true and why.

subject: mathematics
title: Dominoes Equation Game
description: Play a game of dominoes and formulate number sentences while playing.

subject: mathematics
title: True or False Number Sentences
description: Give learners cards with number sentences. Determine which ones are true or false.

subject: mathematics
title: Unknowns at Different Positions
description: Encourage divergent thinking by writing equations with unknown at varying positions: Simple: 12 = 7 + __, 5 + 7 = __, __ + 5 = 12. Complex: 9 + 1 = 3 + __, __ + __ = 3 + 4, 9 + 1 = __ + 4.

subject: mathematics
title: Matching Stories to Equations
description: Give learners two sets of cards: one with number sentences, one with word problems/stories. Engage in cooperative learning to match cards correctly. Examples: "Janai had 12 apples. She ate 3 of them. How many apples does she have left?" → 12 - 3 = 9

subject: mathematics
title: Creating Stories for Equations
description: Create own stories/scenarios/word problems for given equations. Practice reversible thinking by going from equation to story.

subject: mathematics
title: Real-World Shape Examples
description: Use real-world examples to help students understand shape characteristics and properties. Show students a cube-shaped box or a cylindrical can to understand properties of a cube and cylinder.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Story - Author's Chair
description: Allow students to create their own story about shapes and use the author's chair to tell their story to the class.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Shape Story - Sunny the Fish
description: Use the Caribbean story about Sunny the fish who explores the coral reef finding shapes like cylindrical seashells and pentagon-shaped starfish. Discuss the shapes found in the story and relate to local environment.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Making with String and Wire
description: Use string or craft wire to create shapes. Students bend and form materials to make 2D shape outlines.

subject: mathematics
title: Building 2D and 3D Shapes
description: Use ice lollies, matchsticks, pipe cleaners, playdough to make 2D and 3D shapes. Students construct shapes using various materials.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Collage
description: Students make a collage with different shapes and materials. Trace the sides and faces of shapes with their fingers before creating.

subject: mathematics
title: 2D vs 3D Shape Discussion
description: Have students identify the relationship between 2D and 3D shapes. Questions: "What are the differences between 2D and 3D shapes?" "How many 2D shapes combine to make this 3D shape?" "Is a shadow 2D or 3D?"

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Word Search
description: Provide students with properties and they must find the name of shapes in a word search puzzle.

subject: mathematics
title: School Shape Walk
description: Engage students in a shape walk to identify different shapes in their school/community/church/recreation areas. Have students identify a shape and state the uses of it in real life setting.

subject: mathematics
title: Nature Shape Hunt
description: Take students on a nature walk and ask them to find shapes in the environment (circles in tree trunks, triangles in leaves). Describe the shapes found and possible purpose of the shape. Use positional language.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Interview
description: Have students interview each other about the missing shape. Provide opportunities for students to recall facts about properties of shapes.

subject: mathematics
title: My Shape Book
description: Have students make a shape book which includes the shape, properties about the shape, and where it can be found in the environment.

subject: mathematics
title: Virtual Museum Tours
description: Use VR technology or virtual tours to immerse students in 3D environments. Explore museums, historical landmarks, and real-world spaces to identify shapes.

subject: mathematics
title: Digital Geoboard Game
description: Use digital geoboard to create and manipulate different shapes. Students explore shape properties through interactive app.

subject: mathematics
title: Tangram Digital Game
description: Use tangram pieces to recreate different shapes and objects using digital tangram app.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Maze Activity
description: Provide students with a simple maze and ask them to find the path that leads to a specific shape. Describe the shapes seen in the maze and use positional language to describe location.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Transformations
description: Provide students with a set of 2D shapes and ask them to transform them into different shapes (e.g., cutting a square to make two triangles). Use positional language and gestures to describe the changes made.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Transformation Game
description: Provide students with cards showing different shapes undergoing transformation (stretching, shrinking). Describe the changes and use positional language to describe location of shapes.

subject: mathematics
title: Transformation Shape Book
description: Create a transformation shape book showing geometric shapes before and after transformation.

subject: mathematics
title: Design a Shape Maze
description: Students design their own maze to find shapes. Include various shapes along the path.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Video Project
description: Students create a video highlighting shapes found in their environment.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape is Right Game
description: Modification of The Price is Right: Place properties on screen and students must name the correct shape and state the attributes.

subject: mathematics
title: Wheel of Fortune Shapes
description: Spin the wheel and speak about the shape the needle points to. Students describe properties and give examples.

subject: mathematics
title: Musical Shapes
description: Play Musical Chairs and Old Grady on shapes so students' ability to identify and name shapes is highlighted.

subject: mathematics
title: Mystery Bag of Shapes
description: Students are blindfolded, choose a shape from the mystery bag, then give a description of the chosen shape without looking.

subject: mathematics
title: Drawing 2D and 3D Shapes
description: Allow students to draw 2D and 3D shapes. Focus on accurate representation of properties.

subject: mathematics
title: Favorite Shape Poster
description: Create a poster on their favorite shape. Create a poster to advertise their favourite shape and its properties.

subject: mathematics
title: Compound Shape Creation
description: Use one or more shapes to construct a compound shape. Draw a shape using a variety of plane shapes.

subject: mathematics
title: Sand Tracing Shapes
description: Have students trace their fingers on 2D shapes made from sand to identify sides and corners.

subject: mathematics
title: Rolling and Stacking Investigation
description: Allow students to manipulate shapes to deduce those that roll and stack. Provide with toy ramp for experimentation.

subject: mathematics
title: Geoboard Shape Making
description: Students use geoboard to make geometric shapes. Explore properties through hands-on manipulation.

subject: mathematics
title: 2D and 3D Relationship Discussion
description: Have students identify the relationship between a 2D and 3D shape. Discuss how 2D shapes make up faces of 3D objects.

subject: mathematics
title: Tiered Shape Activities
description: Tier 1: Justify the shape appropriateness for its usage in real life. Tier 2: State the properties of all shapes. Tier 3: Classify the given shapes based on identified attributes.

subject: mathematics
title: Geometric Art Project
description: Assign groups to create a piece of art using geometric shapes (2D drawing or 3D sculpture). Encourages creativity, visual-spatial skills, and collaboration.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Walk - Real Life Uses
description: Engage students in a shape walk to identify different shapes in their environment. Have students identify a shape and state the uses of it in real life setting.

subject: mathematics
title: Build a City Project
description: Have students work in groups to design and build a city using different shapes. Use cardboard, construction paper, or other materials to create buildings, roads, and structures. Each group presents their city describing shapes used.

subject: mathematics
title: 3D Object Investigation
description: Provide students with a collection of 3D objects (cube, cone, sphere, cylinder). Investigate and identify different characteristics of each object (number of faces, edges, corners). Draw and label each object in their learning log.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Exhibition
description: Select a 2D or 3D shape and students bring objects from home that reveal their shape. Students make a collage with different shapes and materials.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Exploration Inquiry
description: Start with a question: "What shapes do you see around you every day?" Provide variety of materials (pattern blocks, wooden shapes). Sort and group shapes based on attributes. Record observations and discuss findings.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Collage Project
description: Collect pictures of objects representing different shapes from magazines, newspapers, online resources. Cut and paste onto poster board.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Museum
description: Create a shape museum by designing and creating models of different shapes using clay, playdough, or other materials. Include descriptions of attributes.

subject: mathematics
title: Geometry Scavenger Hunt
description: Divide students into small groups. Give each group a list of geometric shapes and objects to find (rectangle, sphere, cone, cube). Find shapes around the classroom or school. Promotes teamwork and observation skills.

subject: mathematics
title: 3D Bingo
description: Students listen to attributes of geometric shapes and match it with the 3D object printed on their bingo card.

subject: mathematics
title: Wheel of Shapes
description: Names of shapes on large paper with spinner attached. Students spin, identify if shape is 2D or 3D, then state a characteristic.

subject: mathematics
title: Online 3D Shape Games
description: Use interactive online games to practice identifying and describing 3D shapes. Sites: SplashLearn, Education.com, Wordwall.

subject: mathematics
title: Solid Figure Making
description: Have students use cut outs to make solid figures. Students use the given solid figures and make a completely new object.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Tracing
description: Have students trace the sides and faces of shapes with their fingers. Choose a shape and state attributes of shape.

subject: mathematics
title: Roll and Slide Investigation
description: Students are provided with a toy ramp to observe which 3D shapes can roll and which can only slide.

subject: mathematics
title: Pattern Block Creations
description: Students work with pattern blocks and create other shapes or objects out of them.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Property Songs
description: Have students write and sing songs about the properties of shapes. Students listen to songs and view clippings on geometric shapes.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Sorting Activity
description: Divide students into small groups. Provide each group with a set of 2D and 3D shapes. Sort shapes based on specific criteria (shape, color, size, number of sides). Identify similarities and differences.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Patterning Challenge
description: Provide groups with a set of 2D and/or 3D shapes. Create a repeating pattern using the shapes. Extend by challenging critical thinking skills.

subject: mathematics
title: Digital Shape Drawing
description: Use digital drawing tools (drawing apps, online whiteboard platforms) to create representations of 2D and 3D shapes.

subject: mathematics
title: Real-World Shape Analysis
description: Provide variety of real-world objects for students to describe solid shapes. Identify and name 2D shapes they can find in given solid shapes. Example: soccer ball used to identify circle (patches) and sphere (overall shape).

subject: mathematics
title: Design Your Own Shape
description: Students design their own shape using local materials and writing its attributes.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Poster Collaboration
description: Students work collaboratively to create a shape poster showing various shapes and their attributes.

subject: mathematics
title: Finger Paint Shapes
description: Use finger painting to draw shapes. Explore shape properties through tactile art.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Treasure Hunt
description: Treasure hunts involving geometric shapes. Find hidden shapes around the classroom.

subject: mathematics
title: Sand Shape Tracing
description: Use fingers to trace shapes in sand. Students feel the shape while naming it.

subject: mathematics
title: Playdough Shape Making
description: Create shapes using playdough. Use hands to feel different faces of a cube. Create 3D objects using playdough.

subject: mathematics
title: Interactive Shape Apps
description: Use tablets or interactive whiteboards to engage students in identifying and naming shapes. Use an app that allows tracing shapes or rotating a 3D object on a touchscreen.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Attribute Charts
description: Provide students with examples of each shape/object. Have them identify and label the attributes on the charts. Create attribute charts for 2D shapes and 3D objects displaying different attributes.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Impressions
description: Use shapes to make impressions in playdough. Students identify the shape from its impression.

subject: mathematics
title: Attribute Matching Game
description: Create a matching game where students match the 2D shape or 3D object with its corresponding attributes. Example: match a square with the attribute "4 sides, 4 angles".

subject: mathematics
title: Attribute Sorting
description: Provide students with a set of 2D shapes or 3D objects. Sort them into groups based on a specific attribute. Example: sort 2D shapes based on number of sides or 3D objects based on number of faces.

subject: mathematics
title: Attribute Scavenger Hunt
description: Hide 2D shapes or 3D objects around the classroom or outdoor area. Provide students with a list of attributes to find. Example: "Find an object with 4 sides and 4 angles".

subject: mathematics
title: Shapes in Real Life
description: Provide students with opportunities to see 2D shapes and 3D objects in real-life situations. Identify shapes in the classroom or during a nature walk. Identify 3D objects in their homes.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Posters and Flashcards
description: Use posters, flashcards, and real-life objects to help students visualize and understand differences between 2D shapes and 3D objects. Use videos and animations to show how shapes look in the real world.

subject: mathematics
title: Multi-Material Shape Creation
description: Provide variety of materials for students to create 2D shapes and 3D objects (paper, clay, cardboard, blocks). Cater to different learning styles and preferences.

subject: mathematics
title: Shapes in Construction
description: Use pictures of buildings or bridges to show how 3D shapes can be used in construction. Discuss the shapes used and why they were chosen.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Recognition with Visuals
description: Use pictures, posters, and diagrams to help students recognize and name geometric shapes. Use colorful images that clearly show the shape and label it with its name.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Descriptions
description: Provide clear and concise verbal instructions when introducing a new shape. Examples: "A triangle has three straight sides and three angles." "A circle is a round shape with no corners or angles."

subject: mathematics
title: Building Shapes with Blocks
description: Build shapes with blocks. Use pattern blocks to make shapes. Manipulate shapes and explore their characteristics.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Identification Games
description: Work together to identify and describe shapes. Play games that involve recognizing shapes.

subject: mathematics
title: Shapes in Nature and Buildings
description: Use real-world examples of geometric shapes to help students understand relevance and application. Point out shapes in buildings or in nature.

subject: mathematics
title: Touch and Feel Shapes
description: Touch and feel the different sides and angles of a shape. Explore shape properties through tactile experience.

subject: mathematics
title: 2D Shape Rhyme
description: Use rhymes to help students remember shape properties: "Circle, circle, round and round, No corners or edges to be found. Squares are like a box or tile, Four sides and corners make them smile."

subject: mathematics
title: 3D Shape Rhyme
description: Use rhymes for 3D shapes: "A sphere is like a ball or globe, Round and curvy. A cube has six faces, all the same. A cone has one point and a circle base."

subject: mathematics
title: 2D vs 3D Sorting
description: Show students different 2D and 3D shapes and ask them to name each one. Sort shapes into two categories: 2D shapes and 3D shapes. Place circle, square, triangle, rectangle in "2D shapes" section. Place cube, cylinder, sphere, cone in "3D shapes" section.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Bingo
description: Play shape bingo to reinforce shape recognition and naming.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Memory Match
description: Memory matching games with 2D and 3D shapes. Match shapes with their names or attributes.

subject: mathematics
title: Shape Pattern Creation
description: Identify and create patterns using geometric shapes. Explore relationships between shapes through patterning.

subject: mathematics
title: Length and Height Vocabulary
description: Introduce vocabulary for length comparisons: long, longer, longest, short, shorter, shortest, tall, taller, tallest. Use real objects to demonstrate each term and have students practice using the words in sentences.

subject: mathematics
title: Capacity Vocabulary
description: Introduce vocabulary for capacity: full, empty, holds more, holds less. Use containers with water or sand to demonstrate concepts. Students practice filling and emptying containers while using vocabulary.

subject: mathematics
title: Mass/Weight Vocabulary
description: Introduce vocabulary for mass: heavy, heavier, heaviest, light, lighter, lightest. Students hold different objects and describe them using measurement vocabulary.

subject: mathematics
title: Area Vocabulary
description: Introduce vocabulary for area: covers more, covers less, same area. Use tiles, leaves, or paper squares to cover surfaces and compare areas.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Story: The Longest Fishing Line
description: Tell a story about Caribbean fishermen comparing the length of their fishing lines. Use vocabulary: long, longer, longest. Students act out the story and identify length comparisons.
materials: Rope or string of varying lengths

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Story: Grandma's Coconut Water
description: Share a story about Grandma filling containers with coconut water - some full, some empty, some holding more or less. Students identify capacity vocabulary in the story.

subject: mathematics
title: Length Exploration Station
description: Set up stations with objects of varying lengths. Students explore and sort objects by length, using vocabulary: long, short, longer, shorter. Record findings using pictures.

subject: mathematics
title: Capacity Pouring Station
description: Students explore capacity by pouring water or sand between containers. They predict which container holds more before testing, then describe results using vocabulary.

subject: mathematics
title: Heavy or Light Exploration
description: Students hold and compare objects to determine which is heavier or lighter. Use balance scale to verify predictions. Describe findings using mass vocabulary.

subject: mathematics
title: Cover the Shape
description: Students cover flat shapes with tiles or paper squares to explore area concepts. Compare which shapes need more or fewer tiles to cover. Use vocabulary: covers more, covers less.

subject: mathematics
title: Tall and Short Freeze Dance
description: Play music while students move around. When music stops, teacher calls out "tall" or "short" - students freeze in that position. Extend to longest/shortest by having students line up.

subject: mathematics
title: Measurement Vocabulary Bingo
description: Students play bingo with measurement vocabulary words. Teacher shows objects or pictures, students cover the matching vocabulary word on their card.

subject: mathematics
title: Long and Short Song
description: Teach a song about length vocabulary: "Long, longer, longest - watch things grow! Short, shorter, shortest - here we go!" Students use hand motions to show comparisons.

subject: mathematics
title: Heavy and Light Chant
description: Teach a chant: "Heavy, heavier, heaviest - hard to lift! Light, lighter, lightest - like a gift!" Students act out lifting heavy and light objects.

subject: mathematics
title: Measurement Vocabulary Collage
description: Students create a collage showing measurement vocabulary. Draw or cut out pictures showing: long/short, heavy/light, full/empty, covers more/less. Label with vocabulary words.

subject: mathematics
title: Height Lineup
description: Students compare their heights by standing back-to-back. Order themselves from shortest to tallest. Extend by comparing heights of objects in the classroom.

subject: mathematics
title: Length Ordering
description: Provide sets of objects (ribbons, sticks, pencils) for students to order from shortest to longest or longest to shortest. Use comparison vocabulary as they work.

subject: mathematics
title: Capacity Comparison Challenge
description: Students compare containers to determine which holds more or less. Order containers from holds least to holds most by pouring and testing.

subject: mathematics
title: Balance Scale Comparisons
description: Use a balance scale to compare the mass of objects. Students predict which is heavier, then test. Order objects from lightest to heaviest.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Fruit Comparison
description: Compare and order Caribbean fruits by size: mango, lime, papaya, guinep. Discuss which is longer, heavier, or holds more seeds.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Story: Ordering Fish at Market
description: Tell a story about ordering fish at the market from smallest to largest. Students act out comparing fish and using vocabulary: smaller, larger, longest.

subject: mathematics
title: Ordering Relay Race
description: Teams race to order objects from shortest to longest or lightest to heaviest. First team to correctly order their objects wins.

subject: mathematics
title: Guess the Order
description: Teacher displays objects in order. Students close eyes while one object is removed. Students identify which object is missing and where it belongs in the order.

subject: mathematics
title: Area Comparison
description: Students compare the area of different shapes by covering them with tiles. Record which shape needs more tiles, fewer tiles, or the same number.

subject: mathematics
title: String Comparison
description: Use string to compare lengths of curved or irregular objects. Lay strings side by side to determine which is longer or shorter.

subject: mathematics
title: Human Graph - Height Order
description: Students line up in order from shortest to tallest, creating a human bar graph. Discuss who is taller than whom, who is the tallest, shortest.

subject: mathematics
title: Jump Comparison
description: Students jump as far as they can. Compare jump distances using string or markers. Order from shortest to longest jump.

subject: mathematics
title: What is a Unit?
description: Introduce the concept of using objects as units to measure. Demonstrate measuring a desk using hand spans, paper clips, and blocks. Discuss why we need units to measure.

subject: mathematics
title: Measuring with Paper Clips
description: Students measure classroom objects using paper clips as units. Record: "The book is 5 paper clips long." Compare measurements with partners.

subject: mathematics
title: Measuring with Cubes
description: Students use linking cubes to measure length. Create cube trains and measure objects. Compare: "My pencil is 6 cubes. Yours is 8 cubes. Yours is longer."

subject: mathematics
title: Hand Span Measurement
description: Students measure objects using their hand spans. Discuss why different students get different measurements for the same object. Introduction to need for standard units.

subject: mathematics
title: Footstep Measurement
description: Students measure the length of the classroom or hallway using footsteps. Compare results and discuss why counts differ.

subject: mathematics
title: Measuring Capacity with Cups
description: Students measure capacity of containers using small cups as units. Record: "The jug holds 4 cups of water."

subject: mathematics
title: Scoops and Containers
description: Use scoops to measure how much sand or rice different containers hold. Compare and order containers by capacity.

subject: mathematics
title: Measuring with Cubes - Mass
description: Use a balance scale and linking cubes to measure mass. Record: "The eraser weighs 12 cubes."

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Seed Measurement
description: Use Caribbean seeds (tamarind seeds, guinep seeds) to measure mass on a balance scale. Connect to local culture.

subject: mathematics
title: Measuring Area with Square Tiles
description: Students cover flat shapes with square tiles and count to measure area. Record: "The book cover needs 20 tiles."

subject: mathematics
title: Leaf Area Measurement
description: Cover shapes with leaves and count. Compare: "This shape needs 8 leaves. That one needs 12 leaves, so it's bigger."

subject: mathematics
title: Measurement Scavenger Hunt
description: Students hunt for objects that measure a specific number of units. "Find something that is 10 paper clips long."

subject: mathematics
title: Estimation Station
description: Students estimate how many units an object measures, then check by measuring. Record estimates and actual measurements.

subject: mathematics
title: Daily Calendar Routine
description: Daily morning routine using the classroom calendar. Identify the day of the week, date, month, and year. Discuss yesterday, today, and tomorrow.

subject: mathematics
title: Days of the Week Song
description: Sing songs to learn the days of the week in order. Include hand motions and actions for each day.

subject: mathematics
title: Months of the Year Song
description: Sing songs to learn the months of the year in order. Connect months to Caribbean celebrations and seasons.

subject: mathematics
title: Birthday Graph
description: Create a class graph of birthdays by month. Discuss which month has the most/fewest birthdays.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Holiday Calendar
description: Mark important Caribbean holidays on the calendar. Discuss Carnival, Independence Day, Emancipation Day. Count days until special events.

subject: mathematics
title: Introducing the Clock
description: Introduce clock parts: face, numbers, hour hand, minute hand. Students identify parts on demonstration clock and individual clocks.

subject: mathematics
title: Hour Hand Practice
description: Focus on the hour hand. Students show times to the hour on their clocks: 1 o'clock, 2 o'clock, etc. Read times displayed by teacher.

subject: mathematics
title: Half Hour Introduction
description: Introduce half past the hour. Students show and read times: half past 1, half past 2, etc. Discuss where hands point at half past.

subject: mathematics
title: Daily Schedule
description: Create and discuss the class daily schedule. Match activities to times: "We have reading at 9 o'clock." Students show times on clocks.

subject: mathematics
title: What Time Is It, Mr. Wolf?
description: Play the classic game with time focus. Mr. Wolf calls out times, students take that many steps. Practice telling time through play.

subject: mathematics
title: Introducing Eastern Caribbean Coins
description: Introduce EC coins: 1 cent, 2 cents, 5 cents, 10 cents, 25 cents. Discuss values, sizes, and features of each coin.

subject: mathematics
title: Coin Sorting
description: Students sort coins by value. Create groups of each coin type. Count how many of each coin.

subject: mathematics
title: Counting Coins
description: Practice skip counting with coins: count by 5s with 5-cent coins, count by 10s with 10-cent coins. Find total values of coin groups.

subject: mathematics
title: Classroom Market
description: Set up a classroom market with items priced under $1.00 EC. Students practice paying exact amounts using coins.

subject: mathematics
title: Coin Rubbing Art
description: Students create coin rubbings with crayons and paper. Identify coins by their imprints. Discuss coin values while creating art.

subject: mathematics
title: What is a Question?
description: Introduce the concept of questions that can be answered by collecting information. Distinguish between yes/no questions and questions with many answers.

subject: mathematics
title: Asking Good Questions
description: Practice creating questions about the class. Model: "What is your favorite fruit?" vs "Do you like fruit?" Discuss which questions get more interesting answers.

subject: mathematics
title: Questions About Our Island
description: Generate questions about Caribbean life: favorite local food, favorite beach, how do you get to school? Students suggest questions to ask classmates.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Story: Anansi's Survey
description: Tell a story about Anansi the spider who wanted to learn about his animal friends. Anansi asked questions like "What is your favorite food?" Students help Anansi think of more questions.

subject: mathematics
title: Question of the Day
description: Daily routine where students pose and answer a "question of the day." Rotate who asks the question. Collect responses from classmates.

subject: mathematics
title: Question Cards
description: Students create question cards about topics they want to learn about. Use sentence starters: "What is your favorite...?" "How many...?" "Which do you like better...?"

subject: mathematics
title: Partner Question Practice
description: Students work in pairs to practice asking and answering questions. Partners take turns being the questioner and responder.

subject: mathematics
title: Question Ball Toss
description: Students sit in a circle and toss a ball. Whoever catches it asks a survey question. Class helps refine questions to be clear and answerable.

subject: mathematics
title: What is Data?
description: Introduce the concept of data as information we collect. Show examples of real-world data collection: weather charts, attendance, lunch count.

subject: mathematics
title: Shoe Graph
description: Students sort and graph actual shoes by type (sandals, sneakers, boots). Create a concrete object graph on the floor. Count and compare categories.

subject: mathematics
title: Fruit Graph
description: Collect data on favorite fruits. Students place actual fruits or fruit cutouts in columns to create a graph. Count and compare.

subject: mathematics
title: Block Graph
description: Students vote by stacking blocks in columns. Each block represents one vote. Topics: favorite color, pet, activity.

subject: mathematics
title: Favorite Pet Pictograph
description: Survey classmates about favorite pets. Create a picture graph using pet stickers or drawings. One picture = one vote.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Food Pictograph
description: Survey about favorite Caribbean foods: roti, pelau, rice and peas, macaroni pie. Create pictograph with food pictures.

subject: mathematics
title: Weather Pictograph
description: Collect daily weather data for a week. Create a picture graph showing sunny, cloudy, rainy days.

subject: mathematics
title: Introduction to Tally Marks
description: Teach tally marks for counting: one mark for each, cross after four. Practice counting objects using tallies.

subject: mathematics
title: Tally Chart Survey
description: Students conduct surveys using tally charts to record responses. Topics: How did you get to school? What's your favorite subject?

subject: language-arts
title: Show and Tell
description: Regular opportunities for learners to present items and answer questions from classmates.

subject: mathematics
title: Digital Graphing
description: Use online graphing tools to create simple bar graphs or picture graphs. Students input data and see graph created digitally.

subject: mathematics
title: Data Description Vocabulary
description: Introduce vocabulary for describing data: most, least, fewer, more, same, equal, greater, less. Use graphs to practice vocabulary in context.

subject: mathematics
title: Most and Least
description: Using class graphs, identify which category has the most and which has the least. Use complete sentences: "Red has the most votes. Yellow has the least."

subject: mathematics
title: Graph Description Cards
description: Students receive graph pictures and write or dictate descriptions. Use vocabulary: more than, less than, same as, most, fewest.

subject: mathematics
title: Partner Graph Talk
description: Partners take turns describing graphs to each other. One describes without showing, the other draws based on description. Compare results.

subject: mathematics
title: Comparison Statements
description: Using class data, students create comparison statements: "More students like dogs than cats." "Fewer students walk to school than ride the bus."

subject: mathematics
title: True or False Graph Statements
description: Teacher makes statements about a graph. Students decide if each statement is true or false. Discuss reasoning.

subject: mathematics
title: Describing Our Island Data
description: Create and describe graphs about Caribbean life: transportation, foods, festivals. Practice using vocabulary to describe patterns.

subject: mathematics
title: What Does the Data Tell Us?
description: Discuss what we can learn from graphs. Ask: "If we were planning a party, which food should we serve?" Help students see data informs decisions.

subject: mathematics
title: Making Predictions
description: Use data to make predictions. "If we surveyed another class, do you think we'd get similar results? Why or why not?"

subject: mathematics
title: Using Data to Solve Problems
description: Present problems that require using graph data. "How many more students like apples than oranges?" Students use graphs to find answers.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Story: Market Day Data
description: Share a story about a vendor at the market who uses data to decide what to sell. What did people buy most? What should she bring next week?

subject: mathematics
title: Conclusion Cards
description: Students draw conclusions from graphs and record on cards: "I learned that..." "This data shows..." "I think this because..."

subject: mathematics
title: Data Detective
description: Students become "data detectives" who solve mysteries using graphs. Present scenarios and have students use data to draw conclusions.

subject: mathematics
title: How Adults Use Data
description: Discuss how adults use data: weather forecasts, sports scores, shopping decisions. Students share examples of data in their lives.

subject: mathematics
title: Introducing Probability Words
description: Introduce vocabulary: impossible (will never happen), possible (might happen), certain (will definitely happen). Use everyday examples.

subject: mathematics
title: Impossible, Possible, or Certain?
description: Discuss examples: "The sun will rise tomorrow" (certain), "It will snow in the Caribbean" (impossible), "I will see a bird today" (possible). Students sort statements.

subject: mathematics
title: Bag of Cubes
description: Place colored cubes in a bag. If all are red, pulling a red cube is certain. If none are red, it's impossible. If some are red, it's possible. Students experiment.

subject: mathematics
title: Spinner Predictions
description: Use spinners with different colored sections. Discuss: "Is it possible to land on blue?" "Is it certain?" "Is it impossible?" Make predictions and test.

subject: mathematics
title: Coin Flip Exploration
description: Flip a coin multiple times. Discuss: "Is it possible to get heads?" "Is it certain?" Record results and discuss what we notice.

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Probability Examples
description: Discuss probability in Caribbean context: "Will we see the ocean today?" (depends on location), "Will it rain during hurricane season?" (possible), "Will a pelican swim?" (certain).

subject: mathematics
title: Caribbean Story: Fisherman's Luck
description: Tell a story about a fisherman who thinks about what fish he might catch. Some fish are certain (common), some possible (sometimes seen), some impossible (don't live here).

subject: mathematics
title: Probability Card Game
description: Create cards with events. Students sort into impossible, possible, or certain. Discuss and debate choices as a class.

subject: mathematics
title: Probability Corners
description: Label three corners: Impossible, Possible, Certain. Read statements aloud. Students move to the corner that describes the probability of each event.

subject: mathematics
title: Create Your Own Probability
description: Students create situations that are impossible, possible, and certain. Share with partners who must classify each situation.

subject: mathematics
title: Probability Song
description: Teach a simple song: "Impossible means it will never happen, Possible means it might, Certain means it will happen, We're using probability right!"

subject: mathematics
title: Probability Booklet
description: Students create a three-page booklet. Each page shows one probability word with pictures and examples: impossible page, possible page, certain page.

subject: science
title: Sounds Can Be Pleasant to the Ear
description: Show students pictures or actual musical instruments (guitar, drum, oboe) and ask them how the vibration is made.

subject: science
title: Compose a Song or Poem
description: Students compose with the teacher a song or poem about sounds, vibrations, and waves in the environment to practice vocabulary.

subject: science
title: How to Make Sounds With Vibrations
description: Small group experiments identifying how we make things vibrate. Sounds can be made by hitting, plucking, blowing, etc. on different objects and materials.

subject: science
title: Sound Ordering Activity
description: Students put a group of sounds in order from loudest to softest.

subject: science
title: Sound is a Wave: We Can See It!
description: Hold strips of paper or plastic in front of a speaker/radio with volume up, or rest a container of water or hands close to a speaker to observe sound waves.

subject: science
title: Musical Instruments Based on Different Vibrations
description: Make a model of a musical instrument and demonstrate its use using everyday materials.

subject: science
title: Identifying the Origin of Sounds: Can You Guess What is Vibrating?
description: Play a game to identify, name, and classify materials as metals, wood, plastic, etc. based on the sound produced.

subject: science
title: Oboe Exploration
description: Students look at a picture of an oboe and learn how the reed (thin piece like grass) creates vibration when blown.

subject: science
title: Sounds Can Be Dangerous
description: Classroom discussion to identify the danger of loud sounds in our world and how to protect our hearing.

subject: science
title: What if We Couldn't Make Sounds?
description: Demonstration with a rubber band (stretch and pluck) and grass between thumbs (blowing), leading to discussion about vocal cords and introduction to sign language.

subject: science
title: Draw and Cut Light Sources
description: Students draw and cut out sources of light or use light tracers to identify different light sources in their environment.

subject: science
title: Make a Peep Box
description: Construct a peep box to demonstrate that we need light to see things. Students observe how objects become visible when light enters.

subject: science
title: Natural vs. Artificial Light Sorting
description: Sorting activity where students classify light sources as natural (sun, stars, fire) or artificial (lamps, flashlights, screens).

subject: science
title: How We See Objects
description: Interactive demonstration showing that light must reflect off objects and enter our eyes for us to see them.

subject: science
title: Light Source Walk
description: Students walk around the school and schoolyard to identify and document different sources of light they encounter.

subject: science
title: Glass Demonstrations
description: Shine a torch through clear glass, wax paper, and mirrors to demonstrate transparent, translucent, and reflective properties.

subject: science
title: Sentence Completion Activity
description: Students complete sentences using proper terminology: "The window is _____ (transparent/translucent) so we can see clearly."

subject: science
title: Matching Objects to Light Properties
description: Match pictures of objects (windows, mirrors, curtains, wax paper) with the terms: transparent, translucent, reflective, opaque.

subject: science
title: Rough vs. Smooth Glass Comparison
description: Compare how much light passes through smooth clear glass versus rough/frosted glass. Discuss why bathroom windows are often frosted.

subject: science
title: Shadow Making Activities
description: Place hands and various objects in front of a light source to create shadows. Explore how opaque objects block light completely.

subject: science
title: Light Seriation Activity
description: Place objects in order from most transparent to most opaque based on how much light passes through them.

subject: science
title: School Walk - Light Properties
description: Walk around school identifying objects that are transparent, translucent, reflective, or opaque in different locations.

subject: science
title: Warning Sounds Discussion
description: Discuss warning sounds in the community: car alarms, sirens, church bells, doorbells, animal barking. What messages do they communicate?

subject: science
title: Animal Communication Videos
description: Watch videos of animals (turkeys, squirrels) communicating with their young. Discuss why and how they use sounds to warn or signal.

subject: science
title: Make a Siren Sound
description: Students practice making siren sounds and discuss what siren sounds mean (emergency, stay out of the way, someone needs help).

subject: science
title: Simple Bell Signal System
description: Create a bell code: 3 strikes = school starting, 1 strike = lunch time, 2 strikes = school over. Students decide signals for recess and emergencies.

subject: science
title: Making Cup-and-String Telephones
description: Construct simple telephones using cups and string. Experiment with different cup sizes and string lengths to see what works best.

subject: science
title: Light Communication Challenge
description: Use a torch to send messages using quick/slow on-off flashes. Create codes for: "Come play", "Going to bed", "TV show is on", "Did you eat?"

subject: science
title: Animal Sounds Activity
description: Think of sounds 5 different animals make to communicate messages (fear, hunger, happiness). Practice making those sounds.

subject: science
title: Communication Devices Poster
description: Create a picture poster of communication devices developed over the last 100 years: telegraph, lighthouses, Braille, traffic lights, telephones, computers, cell phones.

subject: science
title: Traffic Light Discussion
description: Learn what red, yellow, and green lights mean. Discuss how traffic lights help keep communities safe and organized.

subject: science
title: Morse Code Introduction
description: Listen to Morse code samples and learn that a system of beeps and pauses was developed like an alphabet to make whole words.

subject: science
title: Class Debate
description: Discuss: How do light and sound warnings assist the blind and deaf in our community? How can we improve these systems?

subject: science
title: Mimicking Design Challenge
description: Students design and build a tool to reach something high (mimicking a giraffe's neck) using broomstick, tape, screws, and plastic cup/bottle.

subject: science
title: Matching Activity
description: Match pictures of animals/plants to the technology which mimics them using cutouts.

subject: science
title: Nature Walk - Shameplant Observation
description: Observe the Mimosa pudica (shameplant) in nature and touch it to see the response.

subject: science
title: Shameplant Drawing
description: Record observations by drawing the plant's leaves before and after being touched.

subject: science
title: Shameplant Roleplay
description: Students mimic the movement of the shameplant through the open/close motion of their hands in pairs.

subject: science
title: Flamingo Discussion
description: Discuss how humans mimic the flamingo (stilts for plastering ceilings).

subject: science
title: Defence and Camouflage Discussion
description: Discuss how animals mimic each other for defence and how humans use camouflage (soldiers, hunters).

subject: science
title: Lionfish/Barbed Wire Comparison
description: Compare pictures of lionfish spines to see how humans invented barbed wire.

subject: science
title: Lizard/Suction Cup Comparison
description: Observe how humans mimicked the gecko's feet to create suction cups.

subject: science
title: Which is the Parent?
description: Matching card game pairing animal parents with their offspring.

subject: science
title: Categorizing Behaviours
description: Small groups sort photos into categories: Feeding, Carrying, and Protecting.
materials: photos of animals caring for babies

subject: science
title: Whole Class Discussion
description: Discuss how parents support children using a baby doll or picture of a newborn.

subject: science
title: Human Signals Discussion
description: Students discuss how they signal hunger, hurt, sickness, loneliness, and happiness to parents, and how parents respond.

subject: science
title: Field Trip
description: Visit a farm, zoo, or take a nature walk to observe parent/offspring behaviours in real animals.

subject: science
title: In-Class Video Observation
description: Groups view pictures or videos of parents and offspring in different survival situations.

subject: science
title: Scenario Roleplay
description: Role play scenarios: baby bird chirping hungrily to be fed; baby wanting to sleep and parent comforting.

subject: science
title: Literacy Connections
description: Complete words/verbs for common animal behaviours (E_t, C_y, R_n, etc.).

subject: science
title: Mama Hen Story Time
description: Listen to the story of Mama Hen and the mongoose, then answer response questions about signals and protection.

subject: science
title: Sound Identification Game
description: Listen to animal sounds and identify which baby animal is making the sound.

subject: science
title: Finding Sounds Outside
description: With parent help, students record animal/offspring sounds at home to share with the class.

subject: science
title: Creating Shelter
description: Watch video about animal shelters and build a simple shelter for a caterpillar using coffee sticks, toothpicks, paper, and glue.

subject: science
title: My Favourite Animal Drawing
description: Draw your favourite animal as both a parent and a baby, then present to the class explaining similarities and differences.

subject: science
title: Playdough Modelling
description: Create models of favourite plant/animal as both parent and baby using playdough.

subject: science
title: KWL Chart
description: Track what students Know, Want to know, and have Learned about young animals and their parents.

subject: science
title: Comparing Young and Adult Plants
description: Visit a farm or view specimens to compare mango, tomato, and hibiscus plants using a T-chart.

subject: science
title: Comparing Young and Adult Animals
description: Observe cat/kitten, goat/kid, bird/chick pairs and compare using a T-chart.

subject: science
title: Matching Exercise
description: Match parts of young animals/plants to their corresponding adult parts.

subject: science
title: Drawing a Matching Offspring
description: Look at a picture of an adult animal and draw what you think the baby would look like.

subject: science
title: Video Comparison Activity
description: Watch "Baby Animals That Grow Up to Look Totally Different" twice - first for similarities, second for differences.

subject: science
title: Who Do I Most Look Like?
description: Students bring photos of themselves and their parents to compare characteristics and discuss which parent they resemble most.

subject: language-arts
title: Music Moments
description: Schedule opportunities throughout the day to pause, relax and listen for pleasure with brief selections of music.

subject: language-arts
title: The Sounds of Silence
description: Gather learners to create total silence and listen to ambient sounds; discuss the messages each sound conveys.

subject: language-arts
title: Sound Walk
description: Take learners on a walk around the school to identify and categorize different sounds they hear.

subject: language-arts
title: Daily Read Alouds
description: Schedule daily Teacher Read Alouds with a variety of texts relevant to learner interests.

subject: language-arts
title: Story Response
description: After listening to stories, learners draw or verbally share their favourite parts.

subject: language-arts
title: Prediction Station
description: Pause during read alouds to ask learners what they think will happen next.

subject: language-arts
title: Sound Detectives
description: Play recordings of environmental sounds and have learners identify the source.

subject: language-arts
title: Sound Stories
description: Create stories using only sound effects; learners interpret what is happening.

subject: language-arts
