title: Nature Sounds Journal
description: Keep a class journal of sounds heard outdoors during nature time.

subject: language-arts
title: Music and Movement
description: Play different types of music and have learners move in ways that match the mood.

subject: language-arts
title: Art Appreciation
description: Show artistic representations and invite learners to share what they see, feel, and think.

subject: language-arts
title: Create to Music
description: Learners create artwork while listening to music, then share how the music influenced their work.

subject: language-arts
title: Good Listeners Chart
description: Co-create a "We Are Good Listeners Chart" with positive statements about listening behaviours.

subject: language-arts
title: Listening Partners
description: Pair learners to practice active listening; one shares, one listens and retells.

subject: language-arts
title: Whole Body Listening
description: Teach and practice "whole body listening" - eyes watching, ears listening, mouth quiet, body still.

subject: language-arts
title: Sign Language Introduction
description: Introduce simple sign language as a means to send quiet messages in the classroom.

subject: language-arts
title: Gesture Guessing
description: Play games where learners communicate using only gestures; others guess the message.

subject: language-arts
title: Feelings Faces
description: Study facial expressions and what emotions they communicate.

subject: language-arts
title: Me Bags
description: Provide learners with containers to collect objects representing their interests for sharing during class chats.

subject: language-arts
title: Partner Interviews
description: Learners interview each other with simple questions and report back to the class.

subject: language-arts
title: Feelings Stories
description: Read stories about characters experiencing different emotions; discuss how to respond with empathy.

subject: language-arts
title: Kind Words
description: Create a class collection of kind and supportive phrases to use with peers.

subject: language-arts
title: Role Play Scenarios
description: Act out situations where empathetic responses are needed.

subject: language-arts
title: Morning Greetings
description: Begin each day with greetings that celebrate culture and language; include Home Language greetings.

subject: language-arts
title: Greetings Around the World
description: Learn greetings from different cultures and languages.

subject: language-arts
title: Polite Phrases
description: Practice courteous phrases in both Home Language and Standard English.

subject: language-arts
title: Voice Volume
description: Practice speaking at different volumes and discuss when each is appropriate.

subject: language-arts
title: Expression Reading
description: Model reading with expression; learners echo read with similar intonation.

subject: language-arts
title: Mood and Tone
description: Say the same sentence in different ways (happy, sad, excited) and discuss how meaning changes.

subject: language-arts
title: Sequence Cards
description: Use picture cards to help learners retell stories in order.

subject: language-arts
title: Retell in Home Language
description: After hearing a story in English, learners retell in their Home Language.

subject: language-arts
title: Story Maps
description: Create visual story maps showing beginning, middle, and end.

subject: language-arts
title: Rhyme Time
description: Daily rhyming activities using songs, poems, and word games.

subject: language-arts
title: Syllable Clapping
description: Clap out syllables in words, starting with learner names.

subject: language-arts
title: Sound Sorting
description: Sort pictures and objects by beginning, middle, or ending sounds.

subject: language-arts
title: Word Family Fun
description: Explore word families and create lists of rhyming words.

subject: language-arts
title: Loose Parts Learning Centre
description: Create a space displaying items collected from the environment for vocabulary development.

subject: language-arts
title: Word of the Day
description: Introduce and use new vocabulary words throughout the day.

subject: language-arts
title: Category Games
description: Play games categorizing words (animals, foods, actions, etc.).

subject: language-arts
title: Describing Objects
description: Practice using descriptive language to describe classroom objects.

subject: language-arts
title: Book Shopping
description: Teach students how to select "just right" books from classroom library bins.

subject: language-arts
title: Reading Nook Time
description: Daily independent reading time in comfortable spaces around the classroom.

subject: language-arts
title: Book Talks
description: Students share favourite books with classmates in brief presentations.

subject: language-arts
title: Reading Response Drawing
description: Draw favourite part, character, or scene from a book.

subject: language-arts
title: Turn and Talk
description: Partner discussions about books read during independent reading.

subject: language-arts
title: Reading Journals
description: Simple written or drawn responses to daily reading.

subject: language-arts
title: Author Study
description: Read multiple books by the same author and discuss their style.

subject: language-arts
title: Meet the Illustrator
description: Compare illustrations across books by the same illustrator.

subject: language-arts
title: Our Class Authors
description: Students create their own books to add to classroom library.

subject: language-arts
title: Text-to-Self Connections
description: Share personal experiences that connect to story events.

subject: language-arts
title: This Reminds Me Of...
description: Use sentence stems to articulate connections.

subject: language-arts
title: Connection Webs
description: Create visual webs showing connections between texts and experiences.

subject: language-arts
title: Picture Walk
description: Preview book illustrations to make predictions before reading.

subject: language-arts
title: KWL Chart
description: Document what we Know, Want to know, and will Learn.

subject: language-arts
title: Activate Background Knowledge
description: Discussion to connect prior knowledge to text topic.

subject: language-arts
title: Stop and Think
description: Pause during reading to check understanding and make predictions.

subject: language-arts
title: Think Alouds
description: Teacher models thinking while reading aloud.

subject: language-arts
title: Question the Text
description: Generate questions while reading using who, what, where, when, why.

subject: language-arts
title: Story Retelling
description: Retell story events in sequence using own words.

subject: language-arts
title: Story Map
description: Complete graphic organizer with characters, setting, problem, solution.

subject: language-arts
title: Discussion Circles
description: Small group discussions about the text.

subject: language-arts
title: Character Study
description: Identify and describe main characters and their traits.

subject: language-arts
title: Setting Hunt
description: Find clues in text and illustrations about where and when story takes place.

subject: language-arts
title: Beginning, Middle, End
description: Sort story events into three parts; create visual representations.

subject: language-arts
title: Problem and Solution
description: Identify the problem in the story and how it is solved.

subject: language-arts
title: Sound Boxes
description: Segment words into individual sounds using Elkonin boxes.

subject: language-arts
title: Word Building
description: Use letter tiles to build and manipulate words.

subject: language-arts
title: Blending Practice
description: Blend sounds together to read words.

subject: language-arts
title: Word Sorts
description: Sort words by spelling patterns or word families.

subject: language-arts
title: Word Family Charts
description: Create charts for common word families (-at, -an, -ig, -op).

subject: language-arts
title: Word Family Houses
description: Build "houses" with word family words inside.

subject: language-arts
title: Rhyme and Read
description: Use rhyming to decode new words in same word family.

subject: language-arts
title: Sight Word of the Day
description: Introduce and practice one high-frequency word daily.

subject: language-arts
title: Sight Word Games
description: Bingo, memory, and other games for sight word practice.

subject: language-arts
title: Rainbow Writing
description: Write sight words in multiple colors for memory.

subject: language-arts
title: Sight Word Hunt
description: Find sight words in classroom texts and environment.

subject: language-arts
title: Echo Reading
description: Students echo teacher's fluent reading.

subject: language-arts
title: Choral Reading
description: Whole class reads together in unison.

subject: language-arts
title: Partner Reading
description: Students read aloud with a partner for practice.

subject: language-arts
title: Readers Theatre
description: Practice reading scripts with expression and fluency.

subject: language-arts
title: Caribbean Read Alouds
description: Read stories by Caribbean authors featuring local settings and characters.

subject: language-arts
title: Story Setting Map
description: Identify Caribbean locations mentioned in stories on a map.

subject: language-arts
title: Cultural Elements Discussion
description: Discuss food, celebrations, and traditions shown in texts.

subject: language-arts
title: Kwéyòl/Creole Word Hunt
description: Find and discuss home language words in texts.

subject: language-arts
title: Bilingual Storytelling
description: Retell stories using both Standard English and home language.

subject: language-arts
title: Word Comparison
description: Compare words in Standard English and home language.

subject: language-arts
title: Festival Books
description: Read about Caribbean festivals and celebrations.

subject: language-arts
title: Carnival/Jouvert Stories
description: Explore stories about local celebrations.

subject: language-arts
title: Family Traditions
description: Share and compare family cultural traditions.

subject: language-arts
title: Windows and Mirrors
description: Discuss how texts can be mirrors (reflecting our culture) or windows (showing other cultures).

subject: language-arts
title: Same Story, Different Culture
description: Compare versions of similar stories from different cultures (e.g., Cinderella stories).

subject: language-arts
title: Author Origins
description: Learn about where authors come from and how culture influences their writing.

subject: language-arts
title: I Am a Writer!
description: Create "I Am a Writer" posters; students illustrate themselves as writers.

subject: language-arts
title: Author Celebration
description: Celebrate student writers with an author spotlight each week.

subject: language-arts
title: Writing Territory
description: Students make lists of topics they know and care about for future writing.

subject: language-arts
title: Picture First, Then Write
description: Draw detailed pictures, then add words, labels, and sentences.

subject: language-arts
title: Add-On Details
description: Return to drawings to add more details, then add corresponding words.

subject: language-arts
title: Sketch to Stretch
description: Quick sketches to capture ideas before writing.

subject: language-arts
title: Writing Workshop
description: Daily protected time for independent writing with teacher conferencing.

subject: language-arts
title: Choice Writing
description: Students choose what to write about from personal topics.

subject: language-arts
title: Writing Stamina
description: Gradually build up independent writing time from 5 to 15+ minutes.

subject: language-arts
title: Author's Chair
description: Students read their writing aloud from special chair; classmates respond.

subject: language-arts
title: Partner Share
description: Pairs share writing and give positive feedback.

subject: language-arts
title: Writing Gallery Walk
description: Display writing; students walk around and leave positive sticky note comments.

subject: language-arts
title: Talk Before You Write
description: Partner talk to rehearse ideas orally before writing.

subject: language-arts
title: Story Planning Web
description: Use simple graphic organizer to plan who, what, where.

subject: language-arts
title: Touch and Tell
description: Touch each page while telling the story before writing.

subject: language-arts
title: Sketching Ideas
description: Quick sketches on each page to plan the story.

subject: language-arts
title: Just Write!
description: Encourage getting ideas down without worrying about perfection.

subject: language-arts
title: Brave Spelling
description: Use "brave spelling" (invented spelling) for unknown words.

subject: language-arts
title: Say It, Write It
description: Say each word slowly, then write the sounds heard.

subject: language-arts
title: Reread and Add
description: Reread writing and add details using carets or flaps.

subject: language-arts
title: Partner Feedback
description: Partners ask questions about the writing; author adds answers.

subject: language-arts
title: Teacher Conference
description: One-on-one conferences focusing on one teaching point.

subject: language-arts
title: Detail Hunt
description: Look for places to add who, what, where, when, why details.

subject: language-arts
title: CUPS Check
description: Check for Capitals, Understanding, Punctuation, Spelling.

subject: language-arts
title: Word Wall Check
description: Check spelling of word wall words.

subject: language-arts
title: Publishing Party
description: Create final copies with covers; share with audience.

subject: language-arts
title: Class Book
description: Combine student writing into class books for library.

subject: language-arts
title: Sky Write
description: Form letters in the air using large arm movements.

subject: language-arts
title: Rainbow Writing
description: Trace letters multiple times in different colors.

subject: language-arts
title: Sand/Salt Tray
description: Practice letter formation in sand or salt trays.

subject: language-arts
title: Letter of the Week
description: Focus on one letter's formation with multiple practice activities.

subject: language-arts
title: Sound Boxes
description: Use Elkonin boxes to segment and spell words.

subject: language-arts
title: Word Wall Practice
description: Daily practice reading and writing word wall words.

subject: language-arts
title: Look, Say, Cover, Write, Check
description: Multi-step strategy for learning to spell words.

subject: language-arts
title: Spelling Patterns
description: Learn word families and apply patterns to new words.

subject: language-arts
title: Capital Letter Hunt
description: Find capitals in classroom texts and discuss why they're used.

subject: language-arts
title: Name Practice
description: Practice writing names with capitals.

subject: language-arts
title: Sentence Beginnings
description: Circle first letter of sentences; make them capitals.

subject: language-arts
title: Period Patrol
description: Read sentences aloud; drop voice at period.

subject: language-arts
title: Question Mark Detective
description: Identify questions and practice writing question marks.

subject: language-arts
title: Punctuation Songs
description: Learn songs about when to use periods and question marks.

subject: language-arts
title: Finger Spacing
description: Use finger to check spaces between words.

subject: language-arts
title: Family Portrait Writing
description: Draw family members and write about each one.

subject: language-arts
title: Family Traditions
description: Write about special things your family does together.

subject: language-arts
title: Family Interview
description: Interview family member; write about what you learned.

subject: language-arts
title: Kweyol/Creole Word Collection
description: Collect and write home language words and their meanings.

subject: language-arts
title: Bilingual Labels
description: Create labels in both Home Language and Standard English.

subject: language-arts
title: Family Sayings
description: Write proverbs and sayings used in family.

subject: language-arts
title: Code-Switching Stories
description: Write stories that include both languages naturally.

subject: language-arts
title: Festival Writing
description: Write about Carnival, Jouvert, Independence Day, or local festivals.

subject: language-arts
title: Caribbean Foods
description: Write about favorite local foods and how they're made.

subject: language-arts
title: Island Life
description: Write about living in the Caribbean: beaches, rain, animals.

subject: language-arts
title: Community Helpers
description: Write about people who help in our community.

subject: language-arts
title: Cultural Celebration Display
description: Create display of cultural writing for families.

subject: language-arts
title: Family Read Night
description: Invite families to hear students read cultural writing.

subject: language-arts
title: Class Culture Book
description: Combine cultural writing into class book.

subject: social-studies
title: Family Celebrations Brainstorm
description: Students brainstorm different forms of celebrations practiced in families such as birthdays, weddings, christenings/baptisms, wakes, Christmas, and Easter. Create a class chart categorizing celebrations.

subject: social-studies
title: Cultural Elements Matching
description: Students identify aspects of culture such as food, music, and dance that are relevant to celebrations in families. Match cultural elements to specific celebrations using picture cards.

subject: social-studies
title: Religious and National Festivals Calendar
description: Students list religious festivals like Christmas and Easter, and national festivals like Independence Day and Carnival. Create a class calendar showing when these festivals occur.

subject: social-studies
title: National Symbols Identification
description: Students identify the national symbols of their country including the flag, anthem, pledge, national bird, national dish, and national drink. Create a symbols display board.

subject: social-studies
title: Celebration Role Play
description: Students role-play aspects of family celebrations including traditional foods, dance, and music. Demonstrate different forms of celebration and explain the importance of preserving local culture.

subject: social-studies
title: Pop-Up Book Creation
description: Students create pop-up books about family celebrations including vocabulary and images about foods, dance, and music from their cultural heritage.

subject: social-studies
title: Family Interview Project
description: Students interview family members about cultural practices and how their family, school, and nation celebrate various religious and national festivals.

subject: social-studies
title: Festival Poster Design
description: Students design posters on specific religious or national events. Play matching games connecting aspects of culture with seasons.

subject: social-studies
title: National Symbols Art Project
description: Students describe the national symbols through drawing, painting, and other expressions. Create models of national symbols and draw or color the national flag.

subject: social-studies
title: National Colors T-Shirt
description: Students create hand-stamped T-shirts using national colors. Participate in National Colors Day activities.

subject: social-studies
title: Cultural Heritage Gallery Walk
description: Students participate in gallery walks to observe and share family cultural celebrations. Appreciate their cultural heritage through exploration and discussion.

subject: social-studies
title: Celebration Journal
description: Students journal about their likes and dislikes regarding celebrations. Write or create video journals reflecting on respect for people who celebrate differently.

subject: social-studies
title: Respecting National Symbols
description: Students observe and practice showing respect for national symbols, such as standing correctly during the national anthem and caring for the flag.

subject: social-studies
title: Clean Environment Discussion
description: Students discuss the importance of keeping their home, school, and community tidy and clean. Identify ways to maintain cleanliness in different environments.

subject: social-studies
title: Family Helpers Web
description: Students recognize that when everyone in their family shares and helps one another, they all benefit. Create a web diagram showing how family members help each other.

subject: social-studies
title: Trusted Adults Identification
description: Students identify persons to turn to when they need help. Create cards with pictures and names of trusted adults in home, school, and community.

subject: social-studies
title: What is a Leader?
description: Students understand what a leader is and identify leaders in various contexts including home, school, church, community, and nation.

subject: social-studies
title: Good Manners Exploration
description: Students recognize appropriate manners and behaviors towards adults in positions of authority. Explore respectful ways to interact with adults.

subject: social-studies
title: Trash Can Practice
description: Students practice putting garbage and litter in trash cans at school and home. Demonstrate proper waste disposal habits and participate in clean-up campaigns.

subject: social-studies
title: Family Helpers List
description: Students list the ways that siblings, parents, grandparents, and other relatives help them and vice versa. Create a two-column chart showing giving and receiving help.

subject: social-studies
title: Safety Communication Practice
description: Students communicate ways to express when they feel unsafe. Practice effective communication strategies to express feelings of discomfort or danger.

subject: social-studies
title: Leaders in My World
description: Students identify leaders in their home, school, church, community, and nation. Play matching games connecting leaders with their roles.

subject: social-studies
title: Manners Role Play
description: Students practice using proper manners when interacting with adults. Role-play scenarios demonstrating saying "please," "thank you," and showing respect.

subject: social-studies
title: Clean Spaces Appreciation Walk
description: Students observe and appreciate places in their environment that are kept clean and tidy. Take a walk around school to identify and appreciate clean spaces.

subject: social-studies
title: Helping Hands Collage
description: Students appreciate that helping other family members is important for collective wellbeing. Create collages showing family members helping each other.

subject: social-studies
title: Safe Feelings Journal
description: Students appreciate feeling safe in their environment. Journal about times they felt safe and strategies to maintain personal safety.

subject: social-studies
title: Leaders Help Us
description: Students appreciate that leaders are an important part of our well-being. Discuss how leaders solve problems and support community well-being.

subject: social-studies
title: Respect in Action
description: Students demonstrate respect for persons in authority through appropriate behaviors and manners. Create a manners mobile with words showing respect.

subject: social-studies
title: My Home Address
description: Students identify where their family lives. Practice stating their address and describing their home and immediate surroundings.

subject: social-studies
title: Relative Location Vocabulary
description: Students understand basic terms for relative location: near and far, next to, opposite, in front, and behind. Practice using these terms to describe locations.

subject: social-studies
title: Cardinal Directions Introduction
description: Students name and illustrate the four cardinal directions (North, South, East, West). Create compass roses and understand their use in navigation.

subject: social-studies
title: Family Activities Because of Location
description: Students list activities that they and their family engage in because of where they live. Discuss how location influences available activities.

subject: social-studies
title: Natural Hazards Identification
description: Students identify natural hazards which may pose a threat to their family because of where they live, such as hurricanes, tsunamis, volcanic eruptions, and earthquakes.

subject: social-studies
title: Home and Neighbors Drawing
description: Students locate their home in relation to their closest neighbours. Draw pictures of their home and neighbors' homes, identifying relative positions.

subject: social-studies
title: Near and Far Mapping
description: Students distinguish between and show near and far in their immediate environment. Create simple maps showing objects, people, and places that are near or far.

subject: social-studies
title: Neighborhood Description Practice
description: Students use relative location to describe places in their neighbourhood. Practice giving directions using terms like next to, opposite, in front, and behind.

subject: social-studies
title: Cardinal Directions Home Location
description: Students describe the location of their home using cardinal directions in relation to natural and built features.

subject: social-studies
title: Family Activities Impact Chart
description: Students classify family activities as having a positive or negative impact on the neighbourhood. Create cause-and-effect charts.

subject: social-studies
title: Evacuation Drill Practice
description: Students demonstrate how to follow evacuation procedures in their school. Create maps showing evacuation routes.

subject: social-studies
title: Neighborhood Appreciation Walk
description: Students appreciate that their family lives in a neighbourhood. Take a nature walk to observe and recognize the importance of their neighborhood and the people who live there.

subject: social-studies
title: Location Words Importance Journal
description: Students recognize the importance of being able to describe relative location. Journal about times when location words helped find things or navigate.

subject: social-studies
title: Activities and Location Connection
description: Students appreciate that relative location can influence activities that families engage in. Discuss how living near beaches, mountains, or cities affects daily life.

subject: social-studies
title: Cardinal Directions Navigation Game
description: Students appreciate the value of directions for finding their way. Play the game "North-South-East-West! Choose the one you love the best" to practice navigation.

subject: social-studies
title: People and Environment Relationship
description: Students recognize that where we live influences how our families live and that our actions impact our neighbourhood. Create posters showing this relationship.

subject: social-studies
title: Natural Disaster Safety Poster
description: Students respect the dangers posed by natural disasters. Create safety posters showing appropriate responses to various natural hazards.

subject: social-studies
title: Basic Human Needs Identification
description: Students identify basic human needs such as food, water, shelter, and clothing that are necessary for survival. Create a class chart of essential needs.

subject: social-studies
title: Goods and Services Understanding
description: Students understand that we meet most of our needs and wants through goods and services. Learn that goods are tangible items we can buy and hold, while services are actions people do to help others.

subject: social-studies
title: Environmental Resources Exploration
description: Students identify things in the environment that we use. Recognize natural and man-made resources and understand how these are used.

subject: social-studies
title: Transportation Forms
description: Students understand the various forms of transportation in their local environment. Identify different modes for moving people and goods from one place to another.

subject: social-studies
title: Needs vs Wants Sorting
description: Students distinguish between basic needs and wants. Categorize items as needs (essential for survival) or wants (desired but not necessary for survival).

subject: social-studies
title: Goods or Services Classification
description: Students categorise needs and wants as goods or services. Sort various items and activities into goods (tangible) or services (intangible).

subject: social-studies
title: Using Environmental Resources
description: Students discuss how we use things in the environment. Explain how natural resources like water, wood, and soil are used to meet human needs and wants.

subject: social-studies
title: Transportation Comparison
description: Students distinguish different forms of transportation and their benefits and limitations. Compare various modes (land, water, air) and identify their appropriate uses.

subject: social-studies
title: Appreciating Needs vs Wants
description: Students appreciate the difference between needs and wants. Understand the importance of prioritizing needs over wants and that not all desires are necessities.

subject: social-studies
title: Goods and Services Value
description: Students appreciate that goods and services are necessary to meet our needs and wants. Recognize the value of both in satisfying human needs.

subject: social-studies
title: Environmental Stewardship
description: Students appreciate that the environment can be used to provide our needs and wants. Develop understanding of how environmental resources contribute to well-being and the importance of using resources responsibly.

subject: social-studies
title: Transportation Safety Appreciation
description: Students appreciate the importance of exercising safety around transportation. Value safety practices when using or being around various forms of transportation.

subject: social-studies
title: Class Shop Role Play
description: Students role-play running a class shop to understand buying and selling goods. Practice exchanging goods for pretend money.

subject: social-studies
title: Taking Care of Dolls/Teddy Bears
description: Students demonstrate meeting basic needs through caring for dolls or teddy bears. Practice providing food, clothing, and shelter for their "dependents".

subject: social-studies
title: Goods and Services Poster
description: Students create posters showing examples of goods and services in their community. Draw or cut out pictures to categorize.

subject: social-studies
title: Natural Resources Matching Game
description: Students match everyday items with the natural resources they come from. Connect products to their origins (e.g., paper from trees, bread from wheat).

subject: social-studies
title: Wise Resource Use
description: Students demonstrate conservation activities showing wise use of resources. Practice turning off lights, saving water, and reducing waste.

subject: social-studies
title: Transportation Mobile
description: Students create art projects showing different forms of transportation. Make mobiles with land, water, and air vehicles.

subject: social-studies
title: Transportation Field Trip
description: Students observe different forms of transportation during a field trip or neighborhood walk. Document various vehicles and their purposes.
